# Enhancing Element Style Generation with Page Context

## Current Implementation Analysis

The current element style generation module has several components:

1. **Frontend (Content Script)**:
   - Selects elements using `getSelectedElementDetails` function
   - Appears to collect some contextual information with `getElementContext` (though not found in search results)
   - Extracts simplified page structure with `extractSimplifiedStructure`
   - Applies styles to selected elements with `applyElementStyle`

2. **Backend (Flask Server)**:
   - Receives element details via the `/api/generate_element_style` endpoint
   - Generates AI prompts with `generate_element_style_prompt`
   - Creates styles using AI (Claude or Deepseek) via `generate_ai_style_for_element`
   - Merges styles with existing ones using `merge_styles_intelligently`

3. **AI Prompt Engineering**:
   - Already includes some context like element position, parent elements, and page structure
   - Provides design guidelines for generating harmonious styles

## Improvement Opportunities

While the current implementation already has some context awareness, we can enhance it in several ways:

### 1. Enhanced Page Structure Analysis

```javascript
// Add to content.js
function analyzePageDesignSystem(document) {
    // Extract color palette
    const colorPalette = extractColorPalette(document);
    
    // Extract typography system
    const typography = extractTypographySystem(document);
    
    // Extract spacing patterns
    const spacing = extractSpacingSystem(document);
    
    // Extract component patterns
    const components = identifyRepeatingComponents(document);
    
    // Extract layout patterns
    const layouts = analyzeLayoutPatterns(document);
    
    return {
        colorPalette,
        typography,
        spacing,
        components,
        layouts
    };
}

function extractColorPalette(document) {
    const colors = {
        text: new Set(),
        background: new Set(),
        accent: new Set(),
        border: new Set()
    };
    
    // Sample significant elements
    const elements = document.querySelectorAll('h1, h2, h3, p, a, button, div, header, footer, nav, section, main');
    
    elements.forEach(el => {
        const style = window.getComputedStyle(el);
        
        // Text colors (non-black)
        if (style.color && style.color !== 'rgb(0, 0, 0)' && style.color !== 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 1)') {
            colors.text.add(style.color);
        }
        
        // Background colors (non-transparent)
        if (style.backgroundColor && style.backgroundColor !== 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)') {
            colors.background.add(style.backgroundColor);
        }
        
        // Border colors
        if (style.borderColor && style.borderColor !== 'rgb(0, 0, 0)' && style.borderTopWidth !== '0px') {
            colors.border.add(style.borderColor);
        }
        
        // Box shadow colors
        if (style.boxShadow && style.boxShadow !== 'none') {
            // Extract color from box-shadow
            const shadowParts = style.boxShadow.match(/rgba?\([^)]+\)|#[0-9a-f]{3,8}/i);
            if (shadowParts && shadowParts[0]) {
                colors.accent.add(shadowParts[0]);
            }
        }
    });
    
    // Convert Sets to Arrays and limit to most common colors
    return {
        text: [...colors.text].slice(0, 5),
        background: [...colors.background].slice(0, 5),
        accent: [...colors.accent].slice(0, 5),
        border: [...colors.border].slice(0, 5)
    };
}

function extractTypographySystem(document) {
    const fontFamilies = new Set();
    const headingFontSizes = new Set();
    const bodyFontSizes = new Set();
    const fontWeights = new Set();
    const lineHeights = new Set();
    
    // Extract heading styles
    document.querySelectorAll('h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6').forEach(el => {
        const style = window.getComputedStyle(el);
        if (style.fontFamily) fontFamilies.add(style.fontFamily);
        if (style.fontSize) headingFontSizes.add(style.fontSize);
        if (style.fontWeight) fontWeights.add(style.fontWeight);
        if (style.lineHeight) lineHeights.add(style.lineHeight);
    });
    
    // Extract body text styles
    document.querySelectorAll('p, span, div, li, a').forEach(el => {
        const style = window.getComputedStyle(el);
        if (style.fontFamily) fontFamilies.add(style.fontFamily);
        if (style.fontSize) bodyFontSizes.add(style.fontSize);
        if (style.fontWeight) fontWeights.add(style.fontWeight);
        if (style.lineHeight) lineHeights.add(style.lineHeight);
    });
    
    return {
        fontFamilies: [...fontFamilies],
        headingFontSizes: [...headingFontSizes].sort((a, b) => parseFloat(b) - parseFloat(a)),
        bodyFontSizes: [...bodyFontSizes].sort((a, b) => parseFloat(b) - parseFloat(a)),
        fontWeights: [...fontWeights],
        lineHeights: [...lineHeights]
    };
}

function extractSpacingSystem(document) {
    const margins = new Set();
    const paddings = new Set();
    const gaps = new Set();
    
    // Sample significant elements
    document.querySelectorAll('div, section, article, main, header, footer, nav').forEach(el => {
        const style = window.getComputedStyle(el);
        
        ['margin', 'padding'].forEach(prop => {
            const value = style[prop];
            if (value && value !== '0px') {
                if (prop === 'margin') margins.add(value);
                else paddings.add(value);
            }
        });
        
        // Gap for flex and grid layouts
        if (style.gap && style.gap !== '0px') {
            gaps.add(style.gap);
        }
    });
    
    return {
        margins: [...margins],
        paddings: [...paddings],
        gaps: [...gaps]
    };
}
```

### 2. Enhanced Element Context Collection

```javascript
// Add to content.js
function getElementContext(element) {
    // Get element position
    const position = getElementPosition(element);
    
    // Get parent elements (up to 3 levels)
    const parents = getParentChain(element, 3);
    
    // Get sibling elements
    const siblings = getSiblingElements(element);
    
    // Identify visual relationships
    const visualRelationships = identifyVisualRelationships(element);
    
    // Determine element's semantic purpose
    const semanticRole = determineSemanticRole(element);
    
    // Determine if element is part of a pattern/component
    const patternInfo = identifyPatternMembership(element);
    
    // Get interaction state
    const interactionState = getInteractionState(element);
    
    // Analyze text content
    const textAnalysis = element.textContent ? analyzeTextContent(element.textContent) : null;
    
    return {
        position,
        parents,
        siblings,
        visualRelationships,
        semanticRole,
        patternInfo,
        interactionState,
        textAnalysis
    };
}

function getElementPosition(element) {
    const rect = element.getBoundingClientRect();
    const viewport = {
        width: window.innerWidth,
        height: window.innerHeight
    };
    
    // Calculate relative positions (0-1 scale)
    const relativeX = rect.left / viewport.width;
    const relativeY = rect.top / viewport.height;
    
    // Determine position zones
    let horizontalZone, verticalZone;
    
    if (relativeX < 0.25) horizontalZone = 'left';
    else if (relativeX > 0.75) horizontalZone = 'right';
    else horizontalZone = 'center';
    
    if (relativeY < 0.25) verticalZone = 'top';
    else if (relativeY > 0.75) verticalZone = 'bottom';
    else verticalZone = 'middle';
    
    return {
        rect: {
            top: rect.top,
            left: rect.left,
            width: rect.width,
            height: rect.height,
            right: rect.right,
            bottom: rect.bottom
        },
        relative: {
            x: relativeX,
            y: relativeY,
            zone: `${verticalZone}-${horizontalZone}`
        },
        viewport
    };
}

function determineSemanticRole(element) {
    // Determine role based on tag, attributes, position, etc.
    const tag = element.tagName.toLowerCase();
    const role = element.getAttribute('role');
    const ariaLabel = element.getAttribute('aria-label');
    const id = element.id;
    const className = element.className;
    
    // Check explicit roles
    if (role) return role;
    
    // Check common element patterns
    if (tag === 'button' || element.matches('a.btn') || element.matches('div[onclick]') || element.matches('[role="button"]')) {
        return 'button';
    }
    
    if (tag === 'header' || element.matches('[role="banner"]')) {
        return 'header';
    }
    
    if (tag === 'nav' || element.matches('[role="navigation"]')) {
        return 'navigation';
    }
    
    // Check class and ID hints
    const nameHints = [id, className].join(' ').toLowerCase();
    if (nameHints.includes('btn') || nameHints.includes('button')) return 'button';
    if (nameHints.includes('nav')) return 'navigation';
    if (nameHints.includes('head')) return 'heading';
    if (nameHints.includes('foot')) return 'footer';
    if (nameHints.includes('card')) return 'card';
    
    // Default based on tag
    const tagRoles = {
        'h1': 'heading',
        'h2': 'heading',
        'h3': 'heading',
        'h4': 'heading',
        'h5': 'heading',
        'h6': 'heading',
        'p': 'paragraph',
        'ul': 'list',
        'ol': 'list',
        'img': 'image',
        'a': 'link',
        'input': 'input',
        'select': 'select',
        'form': 'form'
    };
    
    return tagRoles[tag] || 'generic';
}
```

### 3. Improved AI Prompt Engineering

```python
# Enhance generate_element_style_prompt in app.py
def generate_element_style_prompt(element_details, description, existing_style=None):
    """
    Generate an enhanced AI prompt for element styling with rich context
    """
    # Base element information
    base_prompt = f"""
    Generate CSS styles for the following HTML element:
    
    Element Details:
    - Tag: {element_details['elementInfo']['tagName']}
    - ID: {element_details['elementInfo']['id'] or 'None'}
    - Class: {element_details['elementInfo']['className'] or 'None'}
    - CSS Path: {element_details['elementInfo']['path']}
    """
    
    # Add enhanced context information if available
    if 'contextInfo' in element_details:
        context = element_details['contextInfo']
        
        # Add position information with zones
        if 'position' in context:
            pos = context['position']
            base_prompt += f"""
            Position Information:
            - Width: {pos['rect']['width']}px, Height: {pos['rect']['height']}px
            - Position zone: {pos['relative']['zone']}
            - Viewport position: {int(pos['relative']['x'] * 100)}% from left, {int(pos['relative']['y'] * 100)}% from top
            """
        
        # Add semantic role information
        if 'semanticRole' in context:
            base_prompt += f"""
            Semantic Role: {context['semanticRole']}
            """
            
        # Add interaction state
        if 'interactionState' in context:
            base_prompt += f"""
            Interaction State: {context['interactionState']}
            """
            
        # Add pattern membership
        if 'patternInfo' in context and context['patternInfo']:
            pattern = context['patternInfo']
            base_prompt += f"""
            Component Pattern: {pattern['type']}
            Pattern Description: {pattern['description']}
            """
            
        # Add parent elements information
        if 'parents' in context and context['parents']:
            base_prompt += f"""
            Parent Elements Hierarchy:
            """
            for i, parent in enumerate(context['parents']):
                base_prompt += f"""
                Level {i+1} Parent (<{parent['tag']}>{parent.get('id', '')}): 
                - Class: {parent.get('className', 'None')}
                - Role: {parent.get('role', 'None')}
                - Key styles: {', '.join([f'{k}: {v}' for k, v in list(parent.get('styles', {}).items())[:5]])}
                """
    
    # Add design system information
    if 'designSystem' in element_details:
        ds = element_details['designSystem']
        
        # Color palette
        if 'colorPalette' in ds:
            colors = ds['colorPalette']
            base_prompt += f"""
            Page Color Palette:
            - Primary colors: {', '.join(colors.get('primary', [])[:3])}
            - Text colors: {', '.join(colors.get('text', [])[:3])}
            - Background colors: {', '.join(colors.get('background', [])[:3])}
            - Accent colors: {', '.join(colors.get('accent', [])[:3])}
            - Border colors: {', '.join(colors.get('border', [])[:3])}
            """
            
        # Typography system
        if 'typography' in ds:
            typo = ds['typography']
            base_prompt += f"""
            Typography System:
            - Font families: {', '.join(typo.get('fontFamilies', [])[:2])}
            - Heading sizes: {', '.join(typo.get('headingFontSizes', [])[:3])}
            - Body text sizes: {', '.join(typo.get('bodyFontSizes', [])[:3])}
            - Font weights: {', '.join(typo.get('fontWeights', [])[:3])}
            """
            
        # Spacing system
        if 'spacing' in ds:
            spacing = ds['spacing']
            base_prompt += f"""
            Spacing System:
            - Common margins: {', '.join(spacing.get('margins', [])[:3])}
            - Common paddings: {', '.join(spacing.get('paddings', [])[:3])}
            - Grid/Flex gaps: {', '.join(spacing.get('gaps', [])[:3])}
            """
    
    # Add page section and structure context
    if 'pageStructure' in element_details:
        structure = element_details['pageStructure']
        base_prompt += f"""
        Page Section Context:
        - Container: <{structure.get('container', {}).get('tag', 'div')}>
        - Section type: {structure.get('container', {}).get('role', 'generic')}
        - Element is part of: {structure.get('targetPath', [])[-1].get('tag', 'unknown') if structure.get('targetPath', []) else 'unknown'}
        - Number of related elements: {len(structure.get('relevantElements', []))}
        """
        
        # Add information about a few relevant elements
        if structure.get('relevantElements', []):
            base_prompt += f"""
            Notable Related Elements:
            """
            for i, rel in enumerate(structure.get('relevantElements', [])[:3]):
                base_prompt += f"""
                - <{rel.get('tag', 'div')}> with classes "{rel.get('className', '')}"
                """
    
    # Add element structure and computed styles
    base_prompt += f"""
    Element Structure:
    {element_details['structure']}
    
    Current Computed Styles (most important ones):
    """
    
    # Format computed styles by category
    key_styles = [
        'display', 'position', 'width', 'height', 'color', 'background-color',
        'font-family', 'font-size', 'padding', 'margin', 'border', 'box-shadow',
        'text-align', 'flex', 'grid', 'z-index', 'opacity'
    ]
    
    computed_styles = {}
    for key in key_styles:
        if key in element_details['styleInfo']['computed']:
            computed_styles[key] = element_details['styleInfo']['computed'][key]
    
    # Group styles by category
    layout_styles = {k: v for k, v in computed_styles.items() if k in ['display', 'position', 'width', 'height', 'z-index']}
    spacing_styles = {k: v for k, v in computed_styles.items() if k in ['margin', 'padding']}
    visual_styles = {k: v for k, v in computed_styles.items() if k in ['color', 'background-color', 'border', 'box-shadow', 'opacity']}
    typography_styles = {k: v for k, v in computed_styles.items() if k in ['font-family', 'font-size', 'text-align']}
    
    base_prompt += f"""
    Layout: {json.dumps(layout_styles, indent=2)}
    Spacing: {json.dumps(spacing_styles, indent=2)}
    Visual: {json.dumps(visual_styles, indent=2)}
    Typography: {json.dumps(typography_styles, indent=2)}
    """
    
    # Add user requirements with context-specific guidance
    base_prompt += f"""
    
    User Requirements:
    {description}
    
    Design Guidelines:
    1. Create styles that harmonize with the existing page design while implementing the requested changes
    2. Match the site's design language (typography, color palette, spacing rhythm)
    3. Enhance the element's visual role based on its semantic purpose ({element_details.get('contextInfo', {}).get('semanticRole', 'generic')})
    4. Maintain appropriate contrast ratios for text and interactive elements
    5. Consider the element's position ({element_details.get('contextInfo', {}).get('position', {}).get('relative', {}).get('zone', 'center')}) when styling
    6. Preserve the element's functionality and accessibility
    7. Use modern CSS techniques appropriately (flexbox, grid, variables, etc.)
    """
    
    # If existing styles exist, add them for reference
    if existing_style:
        base_prompt += f"""
        
        Consider these existing site styles while generating new ones:
        ```css
        {existing_style}
        ```
        
        Ensure the new styles integrate well with the existing ones while fulfilling the user requirements.
        """
    
    # Output instructions
    base_prompt += """
    
    Return only pure CSS properties without selector or braces, like:
    color: #333;
    padding: 10px;
    border-radius: 4px;
    
    Do not include explanations, comments, or anything other than the CSS properties themselves.
    """
    
    return base_prompt
```

### 4. Intelligent Style Application Strategy

```javascript
// Add to content.js
function applyElementStyle(style, elementPath, styleId = null) {
    try {
        console.log('Applying style to:', elementPath);

        // Find target element using multiple strategies
        const targetElement = findElementByMultipleStrategies(elementPath);
        if (!targetElement) {
            return { 
                success: false, 
                error: "Element not found with any selector strategy" 
            };
        }

        // Create a preview of the style
        const previewId = showStylePreview(targetElement, style);

        // Get page design system to ensure style harmony
        const designSystem = analyzePageDesignSystem(document);

        // Enhance style with design system variables
        const enhancedStyle = enhanceStyleWithDesignSystem(style, designSystem);

        // Store style metadata for future reference
        const styleMetadata = {
            targetPath: elementPath,
            timestamp: Date.now(),
            styleId: styleId || `style_${Date.now()}`,
            designSystemSnapshot: designSystem
        };

        // Apply style with specificity considerations
        const appliedStyle = applyStyleWithSpecificityControl(targetElement, enhancedStyle, styleMetadata);

        // Remove preview after a brief delay
        setTimeout(() => {
            removeStylePreview(previewId);
        }, 1000);

        return { 
            success: true, 
            appliedStyle 
        };
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error applying element style:', error);
        return { 
            success: false, 
            error: error.message 
        };
    }
}

function findElementByMultipleStrategies(elementPath) {
    // Try direct selection
    try {
        const directMatch = document.querySelector(elementPath);
        if (directMatch) return directMatch;
    } catch (e) {
        console.warn('Direct selector failed:', e);
    }
    
    // Try generating alternative selectors
    const alternativeSelectors = generateAlternativeSelectors(elementPath);
    
    // Try each alternative
    for (const selector of alternativeSelectors) {
        try {
            const element = document.querySelector(selector);
            if (element) return element;
        } catch (e) {
            console.warn(`Alternative selector ${selector} failed:`, e);
        }
    }
    
    // Try fuzzy matching
    return findElementWithFuzzyMatching(elementPath);
}

function enhanceStyleWithDesignSystem(style, designSystem) {
    // Parse CSS into property-value pairs
    const properties = parseStyleProperties(style);
    
    // Check if colors need alignment with design system
    if (properties.color && !colorExistsInPalette(properties.color, designSystem.colorPalette)) {
        properties.color = findClosestColorInPalette(properties.color, designSystem.colorPalette.text);
    }
    
    if (properties['background-color'] && !colorExistsInPalette(properties['background-color'], designSystem.colorPalette)) {
        properties['background-color'] = findClosestColorInPalette(properties['background-color'], designSystem.colorPalette.background);
    }
    
    // Align typography with design system
    if (properties['font-family'] && !fontExistsInSystem(properties['font-family'], designSystem.typography)) {
        properties['font-family'] = designSystem.typography.fontFamilies[0] || properties['font-family'];
    }
    
    // Format spacing to match design system
    if (properties.margin && !spacingExistsInSystem(properties.margin, designSystem.spacing)) {
        properties.margin = alignSpacingWithSystem(properties.margin, designSystem.spacing.margins);
    }
    
    if (properties.padding && !spacingExistsInSystem(properties.padding, designSystem.spacing)) {
        properties.padding = alignSpacingWithSystem(properties.padding, designSystem.spacing.paddings);
    }
    
    // Convert back to CSS string
    return Object.entries(properties)
        .map(([prop, value]) => `${prop}: ${value};`)
        .join('\n');
}
```

### 5. Smart Style Conflict Resolution

```javascript
// Add to content.js
function applyStyleWithSpecificityControl(element, style, metadata) {
    // Get existing styles that might affect this element
    const existingStyles = getComputedStyle(element);
    const currentSpecificStyles = getCurrentSpecificStyles(element);
    
    // Parse the style to be applied
    const newStyleProps = parseStyleProperties(style);
    
    // Create a strategy for conflict resolution
    const conflictStrategy = determineConflictStrategy(element, metadata);
    
    // Resolve conflicts and generate final CSS
    const resolvedStyle = resolveStyleConflicts(
        newStyleProps, 
        currentSpecificStyles, 
        existingStyles, 
        conflictStrategy
    );
    
    // Apply the resolved style
    applyResolvedStyle(element, resolvedStyle, metadata);
    
    return resolvedStyle;
}

function resolveStyleConflicts(newStyles, specificStyles, computedStyles, strategy) {
    const resolved = { ...newStyles };
    
    // Handle potential conflicts based on strategy
    switch (strategy) {
        case 'override-all':
            // No resolution needed, new styles override everything
            // Just add !important to all properties
            Object.keys(resolved).forEach(prop => {
                if (!resolved[prop].includes('!important')) {
                    resolved[prop] = resolved[prop].trim() + ' !important';
                }
            });
            break;
            
        case 'preserve-layout':
            // Preserve existing layout properties, override visual ones
            const layoutProps = ['display', 'position', 'width', 'height', 'top', 'left', 'right', 'bottom', 'flex', 'grid'];
            
            layoutProps.forEach(prop => {
                if (resolved[prop] && specificStyles[prop]) {
                    // Keep existing layout property if it exists in both
                    delete resolved[prop];
                }
            });
            break;
            
        case 'enhance':
            // Only add properties that don't exist or significantly improve existing ones
            Object.keys(resolved).forEach(prop => {
                if (specificStyles[prop]) {
                    // Compare values to see if new one is a significant improvement
                    const improvement = assessStyleImprovement(prop, resolved[prop], specificStyles[prop]);
                    
                    if (improvement !== 'significant') {
                        delete resolved[prop];
                    }
                }
            });
            break;
            
        case 'smart-merge':
        default:
            // Context-aware merge strategy
            Object.keys(resolved).forEach(prop => {
                // Keep new styles for visual properties like color, bg color, etc.
                const isVisualProp = ['color', 'background', 'border', 'box-shadow', 'opacity'].some(p => prop.includes(p));
                
                // For layout properties, only keep if they're significantly different
                const isLayoutProp = ['display', 'position', 'width', 'height', 'flex', 'grid'].includes(prop);
                
                if (isLayoutProp && specificStyles[prop]) {
                    const improvement = assessStyleImprovement(prop, resolved[prop], specificStyles[prop]);
                    if (improvement !== 'significant') {
                        delete resolved[prop];
                    }
                }
                
                // Always preserve important accessibility properties
                if (['color'].includes(prop) && specificStyles[prop]) {
                    const contrastRatio = calculateContrastRatio(resolved[prop], computedStyles.backgroundColor);
                    const existingContrastRatio = calculateContrastRatio(specificStyles[prop], computedStyles.backgroundColor);
                    
                    if (existingContrastRatio > contrastRatio) {
                        // Keep the higher contrast option
                        resolved[prop] = specificStyles[prop];
                    }
                }
            });
            break;
    }
    
    return resolved;
}
```

### 6. Enhanced Backend Integration

```python
# Modify app.py to integrate the enhanced context

@app.route('/api/generate_element_style', methods=['POST'])
def generate_element_style():
    data = request.json
    element_details = data.get('elementDetails')
    description = data.get('description')
    url = data.get('url')
    existing_style = data.get('existingStyle')
    style_id = data.get('styleId')
    
    if not all([element_details, description, url, style_id]):
        return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "Missing required data"}), 400
        
    try:
        # Record the time for performance tracking
        start_time = time.time()
        
        # Extract the page design system if available
        design_system = element_details.get('designSystem', {})
        page_structure = element_details.get('pageStructure', {})
        context_info = element_details.get('contextInfo', {})
        
        # Log the analysis context
        app.logger.info(f"Generating style for element with context data: " +
                       f"Design system: {len(design_system.keys()) if design_system else 0} keys, " +
                       f"Page structure: {len(page_structure.get('relevantElements', [])) if page_structure else 0} elements")
        
        # Generate a context-aware prompt
        prompt = generate_element_style_prompt(
            element_details,
            description,
            existing_style['style_code'] if existing_style else None
        )
        
        # Generate style with enhanced context awareness
        style_result = generate_ai_style_with_context(
            prompt, 
            context={
                'design_system': design_system,
                'element_purpose': context_info.get('semanticRole', 'generic'),
                'page_section': context_info.get('position', {}).get('relative', {}).get('zone', 'center'),
                'element_type': element_details['elementInfo']['tagName']
            }
        )
        
        # Process the result
        generated_style = style_result['style_code']
        element_selector = element_details['elementInfo']['path']
        
        # Use smart selector if available
        if 'smartSelector' in element_details:
            element_selector = element_details['smartSelector']
        
        # Merge styles intelligently based on context
        if existing_style and 'style_code' in existing_style:
            merged_style = merge_styles_with_context(
                existing_style['style_code'],
                generated_style,
                element_selector,
                context={
                    'element_purpose': context_info.get('semanticRole', 'generic'),
                    'design_system': design_system
                }
            )
        else:
            merged_style = format_new_style_with_context(
                element_selector,
                generated_style,
                context={
                    'element_purpose': context_info.get('semanticRole', 'generic')
                }
            )
        
        # Save or update style in database
        update_style_in_database(style_id, merged_style, url, description)
        
        # Calculate processing time
        processing_time = time.time() - start_time
        
        # Return processed style
        return jsonify({
            "success": True,
            "style": generated_style,
            "styleId": style_id,
            "processingTime": processing_time,
            "message": "样式生成成功！"
        }), 200
        
    except Exception as e:
        db.session.rollback()
        app
